name: CleanRL (Single-file Deep RL)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth; W&B optional for logging

performance.notes: Single-file implementations; performance comparable to SB3; optimized GPU variants available

agent_readiness.notes: Unique approach: each RL algorithm is a single self-contained Python file — no abstraction layers. PPO, DQN, SAC, TD3, C51, Rainbow, DDPG, and more. Philosophy: simplicity and transparency over abstraction. Easy to read, modify, and extend. W&B and TensorBoard logging. Works with Gymnasium. Published in JMLR (peer-reviewed). Excellent for researchers who want to understand implementations or make algorithmic modifications.

gotchas: Single-file approach means code duplication across algorithms
Less reusable/modular than SB3 for production use
Harder to extend without duplicating code
Not designed for large-scale production deployments
best_when: RL research requiring algorithm transparency and easy modification
Learning deep RL implementations from scratch
When SB3 abstraction layers obscure what you want to change
avoid_when: Production RL systems needing modular code (use SB3)
summary: CleanRL implements each deep RL algorithm (PPO, DQN, SAC, etc.) as a single, readable Python file with no abstraction layers. Unique 'single-file' philosophy for maximum transparency. JMLR peer-reviewed. W&B/TensorBoard logging. Best for researchers who want to understand and modify RL algorithms. Less modular than SB3 for production use — choose based on whether you need transparency or reusability.
